---
title: "A White Noise Approach to Evolutionary Ecology"
author: |
  | __Bob Week__
  | University of Idaho
date: 02/20/20
output: 
  revealjs::revealjs_presentation:
    center: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: true
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{xcolor}
  - \usepackage{tikz}
---

## A few observations

  - Ecological communities are collections of species
  - These species tend to interact
  - Interspecific interactions have consequences:
    - Demographic dynamics (population size)
    - Evolutionary dynamics (trait mean and variance)
  - Ecological and evolutionary dynamics tend to be stochastic
  - How can we synthesize these observations in a single unified framework?

## Branching processes and white noise calculus

    

## Pairwise coevolution

Reciprocal evolutionary response of a pair of species due to an ecological interaction

## How can we measure pairwise coevolution throughout a community?

- Create a model that predicts observed patterns
- Use observed patterns to infer model parameters

# The model

## Communities are collections of species

![](IMG_20191001_133654.jpg){ width=84% }



## These species tend to interact

![](IMG_20191001_133721.jpg){ width=84% }



## Interspecific interactions have consequences

![](IMG_20191001_133834.jpg){ width=84% }



## Interspecific interactions have consequences

![](IMG_20191001_133941.jpg){ width=84% }



## How do we model these consequences?

![](IMG_20191001_132232.jpg){ width=84% }



## With well known relationships

![](IMG_20191001_132318.jpg){ width=84% }



## Fitness is fundamental

![](IMG_20191001_132400.jpg){ width=84% }



## Fitness as a function of phenotype

![](IMG_20191001_132624.jpg){ width=84% }



## Theory of the niche

![](IMG_20191001_134239.jpg){ width=84% }



## Mean trait as niche center

![](IMG_20191001_134308.jpg){ width=84% }



## Resource utilization curves

![](IMG_20191001_134617.jpg){ width=84% }



## Niche overlap

![](IMG_20191001_135024.jpg){ width=84% }



## Fitness as a function of niche overlap

![](IMG_20191001_141413.jpg){ width=84% }



## Competition

![](IMG_20191001_141616.jpg){ width=84% }



## Mutualism

![](IMG_20191001_141724.jpg){ width=84% }



## Predator-Prey

![](IMG_20191001_141757.jpg){ width=84% }



## Prey-Predator

![](IMG_20191001_141842.jpg){ width=84% }


## Other components of the model

- Abiotic stabilizing selection
- Demographic stochasticity
- Mutation (Gaussian allelic)
- Imperfect inheritance
- Evolution of additive genetic variance ($G$)

## Parameters

  - $U_i\dots$ niche use
  - $c\dots$ strength of competition
  - $a\dots$ strength of abiotic stabilizing selection
  - $\theta\dots$ abiotic phenotypic optimum
  - $r\dots$ intrinsic rate of growth
  - $\mu\dots$ rate of mutation
  - $\eta\dots$ segregation variance
    - (imperfect inheritance)
  - $\nu\dots$ variance in reproductive output
    - (demographic stochasticity)
    
## Abundance dynamics

$\frac{dN_i}{dt} = \left\{r\color{#F5793A}{-\frac{a}{2}\Big((\bar x_i-\theta)^2+{\sigma_i}^2\Big)}\right.$

$\left. \color{#A95AA1}{ - c\left(-\sqrt{\frac{b_{ii}}{2\pi}}+\sum_{j=1}^SN_j\sqrt{\frac{b_{ij}}{2\pi}}e^{-\frac{b_{ij}}{2}(\bar x_i-\bar x_j)^2}\right)}\right\}N_i\color{#85C0F9}{+\nu\sqrt{N_i} \frac{d\xi_{i1}}{dt} }$

## Mean trait dynamics

$\frac{d\bar{x}_i}{dt} = \color{#F5793A}{aG_i(\theta-\bar x_i)}$

$\color{#A95AA1}{-cG_i\sum_{j=1}^SN_jb_{ij}(\bar x_j-\bar x_i)\sqrt{\frac{b_{ij}}{2\pi}}e^{-\frac{b_{ij}}{2}(\bar x_i-\bar x_j)^2}}\color{#85C0F9}{+\nu\sqrt{\frac{G_i}{N_i}} \frac{d\xi_{i2}}{dt} }$

## Dynamics of additive genetic variance

$\frac{dG_i}{dt} = \mu\color{#F5793A}{-a{G_i}^2}$

$\color{#A95AA1}{+c{G_i}^2\left(\sum_{j=1}^SN_jb_{ij}\left(1-b_{ij}(\bar x_i-\bar x_j)^2\right)\sqrt{\frac{b_{ij}}{2\pi}}e^{-\frac{b_{ij}}{2}(\bar x_i-\bar x_j)^2}\right)}$

$\color{#85C0F9}{-\frac{\nu^2G_i}{N_i}+\nu G_i\sqrt{\frac{2}{N_i}} \frac{d\xi_{i3}}{dt} }$

# The patterns

## Two sample paths

![](community_dynamics.png){ width=90% }

## Network of interactions

![](interaction_networks.png){ width=60% }

# Inference

## Expressions of niche overalp

$\mathcal{O}_{ij}=\frac{N_iN_jU_iU_j}{\sqrt{2\pi(\sigma_i^2+\sigma_j^2)}}\exp\left(-\frac{(\bar x_i-\bar x_j)^2}{2(\sigma_i^2+\sigma_j^2)}\right)$

- $\left[\begin{matrix}\bar x_i\dots&\text{ mean trait } \\ \sigma_i^2\dots&\text{ phenotypic variance } \\  N_i\dots&\text{ abundance }\end{matrix}\right\}$ DATA

- $\left[\begin{matrix}U_i\dots&\text{ niche use }\end{matrix}\right\}$ PARAMETERS

## Inferring niche use

1. Calculate/collect expected/observed frequencies of interaction:

  - $\rho_{ij}=\frac{\mathcal{O}_{ij}}{\sum_{k=1}^S\sum_{l>k}\mathcal{O}_{kl}}\dots$ theoretical expectation

  - $\hat\rho_{ij}\dots$ observed frequencies
  
2. Solve for $U_1,\dots,U_S$ that minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence:

  - $\mathcal{D}_{\mathrm{KL}}(\hat\rho\|\rho)=\sum_{i=1}^S\sum_{j>i}\hat\rho_{ij}\ln\left(\frac{\hat\rho_{ij}}{\rho_{ij}}\right)$

## Implementation

![](ecol_plt.png)

Sample sizes: $\mathtt{100}\sim(\bar x_i,{\sigma_i}^2), \ \mathtt{1000}\sim N_i, \ \mathtt{5000}\sim \rho_{ij}$

## How can we infer remaining parameters?

  - Using a Bayesian approach called a Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) method

## What is a MCMC method?
  
  - A numerical method to compute posteriors of model parameters
  
  - Markov chain:
  
    - A random walk through parameter space
    
  - Montel Carlo:
  
    - Probabilistic acceptance criteria of sampled parameters
    
  - Requires calculation of likelihood
  
## Approximating likelihood

![](x1_plt_whiteout.png)

## Approximating likelihood

![](x1_plt.png)

## What is left?

- Optimize likelihood calculation
- Implement MCMC
- Extend the whole approach to bipartite networks
- Apply to data on plant-pollinator networks

## Data

```{r echo=F, fig.show = "hold", out.width = "70%", fig.align = "center", fig.caption="Interaction rewiring and the rapid turnover of plant–pollinator networks, CaraDonna et al 2017"}
knitr::include_graphics("caradonna_network.png")
```

"Interaction rewiring and the rapid turnover of plant–pollinator networks", CaraDonna et al 2017

## Thanks!

```{r image_grobs, echo=F, fig.show = "hold", out.width = "70%", fig.align = "center"}
knitr::include_graphics("image.png")
```
